name: VPN services
summary: Consumer VPN subscription services — high marketing claim/reality gap.

evidence.claim: Independent audits of 'no-logs' claims vary in rigor; court-subpoena tests and third-party PwC/Deloitte audits are the credible gold standards. Many VPN marketing 'no-logs' claims have no independent verification.
'Military-grade encryption' (AES-256) is a marketing phrase; AES-256 is the consumer-standard encryption used by most major products, not a premium feature.

criteria.name: server_count
criteria.notes: Sheer count matters less than location + reliability; marketing-inflated figure.

confabulationPatterns.reality: AES-256 is consumer-standard encryption (E2). Every reputable VPN and your bank use it. Not a differentiator.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is 'military-grade' framed as exclusive or superior? It's baseline.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Only meaningful if independently audited (PwC, Deloitte, Cure53) or court-tested (E1). Most 'no-logs' claims are self-declared.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the no-logs claim backed by a specific independent audit report or court case?

confabulationPatterns.reality: VPN hides IP from websites but doesn't make browsing anonymous — cookies, browser fingerprinting, logged-in sessions all defeat anonymity. 'Anonymous' is overstated.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the marketing claim conflate IP-hiding with actual anonymity?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Streaming services detect and block major VPN IP ranges; 'unblock' claims have a ~50% success rate that degrades as services refine detection.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the streaming-unblock claim time-limited or specific to particular services?

counterfeitSignals.action: flag — legitimate VPNs don't offer sustainable lifetime access; often indicates startup with funding shortfall

compatibilityQuestions: Primary threat model: ISP tracking, public WiFi, geo-unblock, or genuine anonymity? Different VPNs optimize differently.
Will you use it on every device in your household? Simultaneous connection limits matter.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Renewal price spike (often 200-400% over intro offer)
